patch.value: CodeFactor parity tooling: local ESLint (unicorn) + complexity + duplication lint to reproduce findings and prevent regressions

To *guarantee* zero findings stay zero, stand up local tooling that mirrors CodeFactor's engines. Today the repo has NO ESLint config, so CodeFactor applies its own bundled default ruleset; the sibling remediation items rely on CodeFactor's UI for the authoritative list.

## Proposed tooling

- **ESLint** + `@typescript-eslint/parser` + **`eslint-plugin-unicorn`** — covers `no-useless-fallback-in-spread`, `no-thenable`, `no-useless-spread`, `no-useless-length-check`, plus core `no-unsafe-optional-chaining`.
- ESLint **`complexity`** rule tuned toward CodeFactor's thresholds to approximate the Complexity category locally.
- A duplication detector such as **`jscpd`** for the Duplication category.
- Add a `pnpm lint` script; consider a `lint` matrix entry in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (mirror the existing `gates` pattern) so regressions fail per-PR.

## Decisions to make

- ⚠️ Adding a repo ESLint config makes CodeFactor use OURS instead of its default — reconcile the ruleset (adopt `unicorn/recommended`?) so local == remote and the grade does not shift unexpectedly.
- Pin tool versions and document the mapping CodeFactor-rule → ESLint-rule so counts can be reconciled.
- Keep the new dev-deps out of the published package (`files` allowlist already excludes config) and ensure `npm pack --dry-run` + `security:scan` stay clean.

## Value

Enables `eslint --fix` to clear the mechanical Maintainability items in one pass, and gives a pre-push signal so the A+ rating is durable rather than a one-time cleanup.

A local lint command reproduces the CodeFactor Maintainability findings (rule counts reconcile within tolerance); ESLint --fix mechanically clears the auto-fixable unicorn rules; a duplication check (e.g. jscpd) surfaces the Duplication category; optionally wired as a CI gate so new PRs cannot introduce CodeFactor findings. Adding the config must not regress existing gates (typecheck, 100% coverage, quality:static, contracts:check).
